message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969
Clicked Start button
We've just deployed a couple of changes into prod earlier but we should be all good
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
Uh oh...here we go again
@Mary Lara-Salvatierra can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
what error are you guys getting?
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
Just got a message from my cousin in London she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
I will start checking the logs now
Could it be your checkout change again @Shay? It broke the platform last week!
The checkout service logs are healthy and the service is working as intended
I see 504 HTTP errors in the payment service which means the application is failing to serve the requests
headsup we have an issue on the online boutique
I need specifics. Which regions are affected and how many customers or orders are impacted? Are we currently losing revenue?
EMEA mostly
Understood EMEA is impacted. I need exact figures—how many customers or orders are affected? Are we seeing a drop in transactions or revenue at this moment?
error 504
Noted on the 504 error. Quantify the impact—how many users or orders are affected in EMEA? Is there a measurable loss in revenue right now?
seems that it is affecting mostly UK
Focus on the UK—how many customers or orders are unable to transact? Confirm if we are currently losing sales and provide an ETA for resolution.
the issue is on the payment platform
Understood. I need numbers—how many UK customers are unable to complete payments? What is the estimated financial impact so far? When do you expect a fix?
regarding the payment issue on the UK market do we have the amount of customers that are using that platform?
I can confirm that transaction volumes from the UK spiked during the flash sale and a significant portion of those are failing due to payment errors.
I need an estimative comparing with yesterday or last week
Compared to the same time yesterday and last week UK transaction attempts have doubled but successful payments have dropped sharply since the errors began.
we are in high season we need a number of how many customers are affected
Based on transaction logs approximately 2300 UK customers have experienced failed payments since the issue started a few minutes ago.
we have approximately 2300 customer affected
2300 customers is significant. What is the estimated revenue loss so far and do you have an ETA for restoring payment functionality?
all based on last week logs
Understood but I need real-time data. Are we currently losing transactions and what is the projected revenue impact if this continues? Keep me updated on ETA for resolution.
I need someone from second line that can check the issue
I’ll review the payment service error logs and recent deployment changes to identify any code-related causes for the 504 errors.
can we revert the change?
My code isn't even being called yet so I don't think rolling back is necessary.
or it will cause higher risk of malfuntion?
Would make more sense to investigate Shay's change first?
Daniel has a point - let's try Shay's change first before touching the payment service
Rolling back my deployment is low risk since it only affects a feature flag but I recommend confirming the root cause before proceeding.
we need to mitigate the issue first since we are loosing transactions
I really don't think it's necessary I did full testing and it passed.
Could it be load related due to the flash sale?
customers are getting the error now so we need to think on a workaround
we are losing sales
The payment service health check is returning 504s and latency has spiked even though CPU and memory usage remain normal.
Given the latency spike and 504s we should consider temporarily routing UK traffic to a backup payment provider if possible.
Despite normal resource usage the payment service is experiencing high latency and failing health checks which is impacting sales.
The 504 errors from the payment service are directly causing the transaction failures for UK customers.
where we can re route the traffic from UK?
We can update the load balancer to redirect UK traffic to our EU payment provider which is currently healthy.
Tanya please do a test before send all the traffic
I'll run a test by routing a small percentage of UK traffic to the EU payment provider and monitor the results closely.
we are doing some test I will inform you shortly
Acknowledged. Keep me posted with concrete numbers and an ETA as soon as you have them.
how long it will take to you to finish the test?
It should take about five minutes to complete the test and gather initial results.
Test will take around five minutes but I will need a little longer to validate all data
Understood. Update me immediately once validation is complete and include impact and recovery status.
In the meantime we received any customer complaint about this issue?
Yes customer support has already received multiple complaints from UK users about failed payments during checkout.
please notify all business owners to communicate with the customers and update them about the situation
Tanya do you have the results now?
I'll notify all business owners immediately so they can prepare customer communications and status updates.
The test traffic routed to the EU payment provider processed successfully with no errors or latency spikes.
Thanks please proceed and send the traffic from UK through EU payment provider
I'm updating the load balancer now to route all UK payment traffic through the EU provider and will monitor closely for any issues.
preliminar test were sucessful we are near to mitigation
Good to hear. Confirm as soon as payments are fully restored and revenue is flowing again. I want clear confirmation before we stand down.
can someone else check the payment platform? I still see the error
it is recovering?
I'm still seeing 504 errors in the payment service logs for UK transactions even after the routing change.
Transaction success rates for UK customers are starting to improve but some failures are still being reported.
dashboard shows a little improvement but still failing
Latency is gradually decreasing but the payment service health check is still intermittently failing for some UK requests.
can we reprocess the payments?
We can identify the failed UK transactions from the logs and attempt to reprocess them once the payment service is stable.
Clicked Start_feedback button\n
